tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: four danger-token rules in pretool-bash-safety.sh matched against raw $COMMAND, producing false positives when tokens appeared in quoted string args to non-shell binaries. The library computes a stripped view of the command that preserves only executable contexts.

tasks_completed.description: Modified hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh: added COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED shim and updated four rules
tasks_completed.changes: Added 16-line Python shim at line 714 that computes COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED via bash_context_strip.py. Updated four rules: rule 715 (killall/pkill), rule 722 (kill -signal), rule 743 first grep (rm/mv danger token), rule 751 (filesystem rm) — all now match against COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. M2 binding constraint honored: rule 743 second grep (workflow-path check) stays on raw COMMAND.
tasks_completed.rationale: Minimum-diff approach: only the four identified false-positive rules needed updating. All Layer 1.A-E daemon-restart blocks and absolute-ban rules remain on raw COMMAND.
TDD protocol: tests cover all 5 passes (control-op split, sh/bash -c recursion, heredoc classification, non-shell arg stripping, comment stripping) plus fail-safe contract. All 23 pass.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — pre-existing design flaw (rules always matched raw $COMMAND string)
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Safety rules using grep -qE against the full raw command string cannot distinguish between danger tokens that appear as executable command words vs tokens that appear as string content inside quoted arguments to non-shell binaries.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Compute COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED view that strips quoted string args to non-shell commands (preserving $() substitutions), sh/bash -c payloads (recursively exposed), heredoc bodies (classified by consumer), and shell comments. Four danger-token rules now operate on the stripped view.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: The library (503 lines) implements a recursive shell-context parser with 5 passes. This complexity is inherent to the requirement: correctly classifying quoted string contexts in arbitrary shell commands requires parsing quotes, $() substs, heredoc openers, and control operators. The alternative (simple regex) is exactly what caused the false-positive bug. The test file (210 lines) covers all 5 passes with 23 tests. The hook modification is only 29 net lines.

dev.qa_notes: The four updated rules are: line 732 (killall/pkill), line 741 (kill -signal), line 765 first grep (rm/mv workflow), line 776 (filesystem rm). All use COMMAND_CONTEXT_STRIPPED. Layer 1.A-E (lines 555-626) and absolute bans (lines 632-651) remain on raw COMMAND. AC15 (systemctl restart happy-daemon-dev) and AC16 (cat session_dirs.txt) fire before stripping — verify these remain blocked. The confirmed real incident case is AC13: codex exec with killall+claude in quoted string arg — verify it now exits 0.

blast_radius_declarations.reason: behavioral_test_only:true — hook behavior validated via Python grep-simulation covering all 16 ACs; direct invocation blocked by hook itself during dev cycle. QA should run canary-verify or equivalent behavioral test.

self_verification.notes: python3 -m py_compile: both new .py files compile clean. python3 -m pytest hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py: 23/23 pass. Python grep-simulation of all 16 ACs: 13 testable ACs pass (AC11/AC15/AC16 verified via code analysis — they fire on raw COMMAND before stripping). The AC13 incident case (codex exec with killall+claude in quoted string) now strips to 'codex exec ""' — rule 715 does not match.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex exec blocked by pretool-git-privilege-guard.py hook (hook intercepts codex's internal git commit attempt). Two invocation attempts both blocked with: 'BLOCKED: agent git commit - only the blessed /merge auto-bulk bridge or the /commit wrapper may commit from an agent context.'
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: self-review substituted — 5 adversarial questions: (1) fail-safe import protection verified via || echo "$COMMAND" pattern; (2) AC7 cat<<EOF|bash heredoc detection verified — _process_heredocs runs before _split_top_level, sees bash on same line; (3) env var expansion ($VAR) preserved conservatively (word tokens not stripped); (4) Layer 1.A regression AC15 confirmed safe — fires before shim at line 559; (5) M2 binding constraint on rule 743 verified — second grep on raw COMMAND.

test_bodies_implementation_notes: Hook reads JSON from stdin as {tool_name, tool_input: {command}}. Dispatch prompt described CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT env-var pattern which does not match the hook protocol (hook reads from stdin via INPUT=$(cat)). Each test sends the correct stdin payload using subprocess.run(input=payload.encode()). Multiline commands (AC6, AC7, AC12) use Python string literals with \n — json.dumps serializes these as JSON escaped newlines, matching what the hook receives from Claude Code in production.
recommendations: QA should verify AC13 live: CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT='{"tool_name":"Bash","tool_input":{"command":"codex exec \"The hook documentation says killall and claude processes are monitored\""}}' bash hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh — must exit 0
QA should verify AC1, AC3, AC15, AC16 still block (Layer 1.A and absolute bans unaffected)
QA should verify AC17/AC18/AC19: killall "happy", pkill -f "claude", kill "-9" 1234 all exit 2
Consider adding bash_context_strip.py to any future static analysis or fuzz-testing pipeline since it handles adversarial shell input

remediation_summary.blocker_addressed: B1 — bash_context_strip.py stripped quoted args to danger commands (killall/pkill/kill/rm/mv), creating bypass for quoted-arg forms like killall "happy"
remediation_summary.root_cause_of_regression: DANGER_COMMANDS were treated as ordinary non-shell binaries in _process_segment(); their quoted args were stripped to empty string, preventing hook patterns from matching
remediation_summary.fix_description: Added DANGER_COMMANDS frozenset. In _process_segment(), when cmd_word is in DANGER_COMMANDS, iterate tokens and unquote (not strip) their quoted args — expose content without surrounding quotes so patterns like (killall|pkill)\s+.*(happy|claude|docker) and (kill)[ \t]+- still match.
remediation_summary.test_results_after_remediation: 30/30 unit tests pass, 19/19 AC tests pass (16 original + 3 new AC17/AC18/AC19)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: codex exec blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook — the PROMPT variable contained 'killall "happy"' text which triggered the killall/pkill pattern on the raw command string. This is a known limitation: the hook fires on the shell assignment command, not just codex exec itself. Self-review substituted.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Self-review (5 adversarial questions): (1) path-prefix bypass via /usr/bin/killall — _basename() strips it, handled correctly; (2) malformed single-char quote with len<2 — guard prevents index error; (3) echo "killall is bad" still stripped (echo not in DANGER_COMMANDS) — verified; (4) rm "target" unquoted correctly — rm pattern only checks command word, not target; (5) AC10 regression check (echo containing kill in quoted arg) — echo still strips its args, AC10 still passes. No issues found.
